seoTitle: Corny Point vs Coromandel Valley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Corny Point and Coromandel Valley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Corny Point vs Coromandel Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Corny Point and Coromandel Valley.
heading: Corny Point vs Coromandel Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Corny Point, South Australia and Coromandel Valley, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coromandel Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Corny Point (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Corny Point or Coromandel Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Corny Point has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Coromandel Valley scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Corny Point scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Coromandel Valley scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Corny Point or Coromandel Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coromandel Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
